title: Anime Index – Developer Wiki
description: Personal anime storytelling platform where users catalog anime, write episode-by-episode story breakdowns with images and video clips, and organize entries with a tagging system.

sections.title: Project Overview
sections.description: High-level summary of what Anime Index is, its purpose, and key features.

sections.title: Architecture & Tech Stack
sections.description: Explains the Next.js 16 App Router architecture, TypeScript setup, Tailwind CSS v4 configuration, Prisma ORM integration, and SQLite database.

sections.title: Database Schema
sections.description: Covers the Prisma schema: User, Session, Passkey, Anime, Episode, Media, and Tag models, their relationships, and migration workflow.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.description: Session-based auth using OTP email login and WebAuthn/Passkey support. Covers middleware-enforced route protection, session tokens, and registration/login flows.

sections.title: API Routes
sections.description: All REST-style API handlers under src/app/api/. Documents endpoints for anime CRUD, episode management, media uploads, tag management, and shareable stats.

sections.title: Pages & UI
sections.description: Overview of all user-facing pages: home/landing, dashboard (anime list), anime detail & episode viewer, discover feed, organize (drag-and-drop), watch tracker, and news.

sections.title: Shared Components
sections.description: Reusable React components: AnimeCard, MediaGallery, MediaUpload, Navbar, and TagBadge.

sections.title: Library Utilities
sections.description: Shared server-side utilities: db.ts (Prisma client singleton) and auth.ts (session helpers).

sections.title: Configuration & Environment
sections.description: Environment variables (DATABASE_URL, auth secrets), ESLint flat config, path aliases (@/*), and build/dev commands.

conventions.orm: Prisma with PostgreSQL (dev: SQLite via DATABASE_URL)
conventions.apiConvention: App Router route handlers (route.ts) under src/app/api/
conventions.authMechanism: Cookie-based session tokens + OTP email + WebAuthn Passkeys
